generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Components
instance StyrofoamInsulationBox : DTDFVocab:PhysicalComponent [
    base:desc "Insulated styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to minimize heat loss in the controlled environment"
]

instance Heatbed : DTDFVocab:PhysicalComponent [
    base:desc "Primary heating element within the incubator system"
]

instance IncubatorFan : DTDFVocab:PhysicalComponent [
    base:desc "Fan distributing heat uniformly inside the styrofoam box"
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller running on Raspberry Pi implementing bang-bang control algorithm for temperature regulation"
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller implementing configurable bang-bang control logic with parameters: LL (Lower Limit), UL (Upper Limit), H (Heating Duration), C (Waiting Duration)"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "First temperature sensor inside the styrofoam box monitoring internal temperature readings"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second temperature sensor inside the styrofoam box monitoring internal temperature readings"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor outside the styrofoam box monitoring ambient temperature"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTempData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction - Data Transmission
instance InternalTempData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTempData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTempData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance PeriodicTemperatureUploads : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:triggerMechanism "Periodic readings via low-level driver"
]

// C5: Data
instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature readings from internal sensors (in unspecified units, inferred as Celsius/Degrees)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTempData1, InternalTempData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Ambient temperature readings outside the styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTempData
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Complete temperature data stream including all sensor readings"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PeriodicTemperatureUploads
]

// C10: Models
instance ThermalModelA : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal model with 2 free parameters for predicting internal temperature dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterImplementation
]

instance ThermalModelB : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal model with 4 free parameters better capturing heatbed inertia for state estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterImplementation
]

// C11: Enablers
instance LowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-level driver abstracting sensor/actuator communication with safety protocols"
    DTDFVocab:enables PeriodicTemperatureUploads
]

instance RabbitMQCommunicationServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker implementing RabbitMQ protocol for inter-component communication"
]

instance PythonSimulationFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom-built or open-source Python-based co-simulation framework for model development"
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing real-time sensor data for visualization (implied to be Grafana/InfluxDB)"
]

instance KalmanFilterImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter implementation for state estimation and anomaly detection"
]

instance BangBangControllerParameters : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Configurable parameters for bang-bang controller: LL (Lower Limit), UL (Upper Limit), H (Heating Duration), C (Waiting Duration)"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of incubator status via time-series database and dashboard"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureTrendsInsight
]

instance ModelPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Temperature prediction using ThermalModelA and ThermalModelB"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperaturePredictionInsight
]

instance StateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Heatbed state estimation using Kalman filter with unmeasured temperature estimation"
    DTDFVocab:provides HeatbedStateInsight
]

instance ControllerOptimization : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "What-if simulations to optimize controller parameters (H, C, LL, UL) using co-simulation tools"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Self-adaptation loop for dynamic reconfiguration of controller and model calibration"
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicReconfigurationAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Kalman filter-based anomaly detection identifying deviations from expected behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureTrendsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of temperature trends within the incubator system"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of sudden temperature deviations from expected behavior via Kalman filter"
]

instance TemperaturePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted internal temperature using thermal models with varying accuracy"
]

instance HeatbedStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimated heatbed temperature despite missing sensor data using state estimation algorithms"
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of controller parameters (H, C, LL, UL) through what-if simulations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DynamicReconfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated reconfiguration of controller and model calibration based on detected anomalies"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C13: Environment
instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains StyrofoamInsulationBox, Heatbed, IncubatorFan,
             RaspberryPiController, BangBangController,
             InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance CyberPhysicalSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LowLevelDriver, RabbitMQCommunicationServer,
             PythonSimulationFramework, TimeSeriesDatabase,
             KalmanFilterImplementation, BangBangControllerParameters,
             TemperatureDataStream, InternalTemperatureData, ExternalTemperatureData
]


instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    **Control Commands & Flow:**
    - **Command Types:**
      - Control signals for the **fan** and **heatbed** (Document 5, 6).
      - **Heating Signal:** Controller issues a heating command to turn on the heatbed until the measured temperature reaches the reference (Document 5, 8).
      - **Waiting Duration:** Controller enforces a delay post-actuation to prevent overshoot (Document 5, 8).

    **Communication Protocol & Validation:**
    - **Protocol:** RabbitMQ (Document 5, 6).
    - **Message Flow:** Controller sends commands via RabbitMQ to the low-level driver (Document 6).
    - **Feedback Mechanism:** Controller receives measured temperature data from sensors via RabbitMQ (Document 6).
    - **Validation:** No explicit authentication or message format validation mentioned; inferred as implicit trust in RabbitMQ’s message reliability (Document 6).

    **Execution Confirmation:**
    - Low-level driver executes commands and uploads system states to RabbitMQ (Document 6).
    - No explicit confirmation mechanism mentioned, but inferred from the state synchronization (Document 6).
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    - **Low-level driver temperature reading frequency**: Periodic (unspecified exact interval) – inferred from 'periodically reads the temperature data' (Doc 6).
    - **Controller execution latency**: Immediate after receiving temperature measurements (Doc 6).
    - **Data synchronization via RabbitMQ**: Latency requirements not explicitly quantified, but RabbitMQ is described as a lightweight, real-time-capable message broker (Doc 6). Assumed minimal latency (~10-30 seconds) for critical control loops based on typical CPS implementations.
    - **Temporal granularity for monitoring**: Real-time visualization of temperature data (Doc 5) implies sub-second granularity for dashboard updates.
    - **Calibration process timing**: On-demand calibration runs automatically (Doc 5) with unspecified but likely sub-minute execution time.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    - **Architecture Pattern**:
      - **CPS-Based DT Framework**: Combines cyber (software: controller, RabbitMQ, Kalman filter) and physical (PT: hardware sensors/actuators) components, as depicted in fig. 1 (CPS-based DT schematic).
      - **Orchestration**:
        - **Calibration Algorithms**: DT uses Model A/B to update parameters via on-demand calibration (e.g., cooling/heating experiments).
        - **Monitoring**: DT monitors PT performance via sensors (temperature) and triggers reconfigurations (e.g., controller updates) when anomalies detected (Kalman filter).
        - **Self-Adaptation Loop**: Implemented as a multi-step process (fig. 5):
          1. Anomaly detection → 2. Data collection → 3. Model calibration → 4. Kalman filter reconfiguration → 5. What-if simulations → 6. Controller reconfiguration.

    - **Component Integration**:
      - **Low-Level Driver**: Abstracts PT communication (sensors/actuators) and uploads data to RabbitMQ.
      - **Controller**: Runs on Raspberry Pi, listens to RabbitMQ for commands, and controls fan/heatbed via low-level driver.
      - **Communication Server (RabbitMQ)**: Facilitates real-time message passing between PT and DT components (e.g., controller ↔ low-level driver ↔ DT services).
      - **DT Services**:
        - **Visualization**: Dashboard (time-series database + plots).
        - **Anomaly Detection**: Kalman filter (Model B + sensor data).
        - **What-If Simulations**: Co-simulation with historical data.

    - **Resource Management**:
      - **Hardware**: Raspberry Pi (embedded controller) + sensors/actuators.
      - **Software**: RabbitMQ (message broker), time-series database (for visualization), and custom DT algorithms (Kalman filter, model calibration).
  "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    **Development Phases:**
    - **Model Development:**
      - Abstract models are created to represent system behavior (e.g., CAD models for static properties like wheel sizes, chassis shapes, or thermal models for temperature dynamics).
      - Dynamic models (e.g., differential equations) are built for specific aspects like force dynamics or thermal inertia (Document 1).
      - Calibration methods are applied to refine models (e.g., Model A vs. Model B for the incubator’s heatbed inertia, Document 5).
      - Models are deployed as part of a Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and integrated into the Digital Twin (DT) framework upon system deployment (Document 1).

    **Lifecycle Integration:**
    - **Deployment Transition:**
      - Post-deployment, models are retained and integrated into the DT to enable continuous monitoring, simulation, and optimization (Document 1).
      - The DT framework persists beyond the initial deployment phase, incorporating real-time data, models, and algorithms (Document 1).
    - **Lifecycle Governance:**
      - The incubator case study uses an open-source repository ([GitHub](https://github.com/INTO-CPS-Association/example-incubator)) for version-controlled hardware/software specifications, enabling collaborative development and reproducibility (Document 2).
      - Future work includes iterative calibration (e.g., Model B’s adaptation for new thermal conditions, Document 5).
  "
]
